rows.description: Computus Dni Rectoria I L Richards
rows.notes: Top title line is legible though a couple of letters (Dni, I L Richards) are in ornate script. No amounts appear on this line. Vertical ruling not involved.

rows.description: a 2d die Novr AD 1824 ad eundem diem resolution 1824
rows.notes: Second header line is partly faded and compacted; reading is plausible but some words are abbreviations (2d die, Novr, AD). No monetary figures on the same line.
Centered section heading; clearly separated from amounts. Spelling follows manuscript orthography.
Clear, centered heading introducing the following fixed rents; no amounts on this line.
Number '60' is clearly aligned in the pounds column (left-most of the three monetary columns). No shillings or pence indicated. Vertical ruling for pounds is unambiguous here.
'10' sits in the pounds column similar to the prior line. Handwriting of 'Kidlington' is slightly compressed but amount alignment is unambiguous.

Description partly uncertain: the manuscript reads 'Do' then a two-word name that looks like 'Capras & Beaul' (letters faded/connected). Amount columns read clearly as '1' (pounds), '7' (shillings), '6' (pence). Chose this reading though the place-name spelling is uncertain.
Amount appears as three columns '9 6 8' which align cleanly with the three vertical rules. 'Lad Lane late Norris' reading is fairly clear though 'Lad' might be 'Lade'. Chosen based on letter shapes.
Description likely 'Chaplin 15. midn.' (some abbreviation). Monetary columns read '6 13 4' aligned under pounds/shillings/pence respectively. Slight uncertainty in the abbreviation 'midn.'
Description 'Pitford 13' is legible; amount columns show '1 13 4'. The year/number after Pitford is faint but does not affect the amounts which align well.
Place-name is somewhat uncertain (reads 'Medersham' or 'Nederham'); however amounts '16 13 4' are clearly placed in the pounds/shillings/pence columns. Chose 'Medersham' as closest visual match.
Description is densely written; reads approximately as 'Littleton Colman late Bucks 15 yr'. Amount columns read '1 6 1' clearly. Lower confidence due to uncertainty in exact place-name wording.

Description looks like 'Lymouth - 3 yr'. The numeric appears as two columns '17 6' (pounds and shillings) with no pence in the pence column. Alignment implies pounds=17, shillings=6. Slight uncertainty over the place spelling.
Line is heavily struck through and the text is crossed out; appears to be 'Brockley Frome' or similar. No amounts visible (struck and/or omitted). Low confidence due to crossing-out and faded ink.
Centered section heading clearly written; separates the prior block from the redeemed land tax entries.
Amount '11 2 9' is clearly aligned in the three monetary columns. The description shows 'Kidlington Rectory' with a faint '1? 2 yr' abbreviation; reading of the date/term is slightly uncertain.
'28' is placed clearly in the pounds column. 'Long Wittenham Rectory' inscription is legible; there is no shilling or pence notation for this line.
Reading of place 'Garston Rectory' is clear; the numeric '14' sits in the pounds column. There is no shillings/pence on this line. Slight ambiguity in the small trailing mark after '13'.
Section heading clearly written (centered). Introduces the following variable rents.

Amount '91 2 6' is clear and correctly fits pounds/shillings/pence columns. Description mostly legible; a small numeric abbreviation after 'Augmentation' is faint but does not affect the monetary reading.
Amount '102 7 5' is well-aligned in the three monetary columns. Description matches prior 'Long Wittenham' notation; minor uncertainty in abbreviated year marker but amounts are unambiguous.

Amount reads '171 11 4 1/2' (pence shows a halfpenny symbol). Columns are well aligned. Description 'St Newington Rectory' is legible; numeric after it is slightly uncertain but does not affect monetary extraction.
Amount '7 10 2' is clearly aligned. 'Do Tenement 3 yr' (i.e., 'ditto') is readable and corresponds to the previous St Newington entry.

Large amount '238 15 10' is clearly written and aligns with pounds/shillings/pence. Description includes 'Tax deducted' note which is legible; the small numeric after 'Rectory' is faint but does not impact amounts.
'44 9 4' is readable and in the correct columns. 'Garston Rectory' matches the earlier Garston entry; a trailing mark after '15' is faint but not relevant to the amounts.

Bottom total is boxed/ruled and reads '806 17 1/2' clearly. Alignment with the three monetary columns is unambiguous and pence fraction identified as a halfpenny (0.5). Description line is blank in the manuscript (just the total).
